headline: Italy first agent baseline. February 2026 cabinet approved migration legislation: naval blockades of migrant boats; 30-day renewable bans without protection-needs assessment; humanitarian SAR NGOs criminalized with 50,000 euro fines and repeated-offense boat seizures. Italy co-funds Libyan and Tunisian coast guards with documented HR abuse records. HRW: 'Puts Lives at Risk.' Albania offshore detention plan repeatedly rejected by Italian courts. Compound migration-policy evidence supports modest downgrade pressure on BND (3.5 → 3.0) and INT (3.0 → 2.5) — naval-blockade authority crosses fundamental boundaries on refoulement and humanitarian-rescue obligations; declared EU/CoE rights commitments contradicted by criminalization of rescue. Composite reconstructs to ~46.9. Functional band sustained. Recommend downgrade.
boundary_note: Sub-threshold delta (-3.1 points). Confirm at 50.0 with downward dimension pressure notation. Boundary-case approved: medium-confidence evidence of BND/INT erosion does not yet cross 5-point material threshold. Re-evaluate within 60 days as Feb 2026 bill operationalizes.
key_evidence: Feb 2026 cabinet-approved migration bill: naval blockades of migrant boats; 30-day renewable bans without protection-needs assessment.
Bill criminalizes humanitarian SAR NGOs: fines up to 50,000 euros; repeated-offense boat seizures.
Italy co-funds Libyan and Tunisian coast guards with documented HR abuse records (HRW).
Albania offshore detention plan: repeated rejections by Italian courts.
HRW Feb 13 2026: 'Italy's Harsh Immigration Bill Puts Lives at Risk.'
Milan municipal pushback against PM Meloni's immigration policies (NPR Feb 22).

evidence.source: HRW Feb 13 2026 — Italy's harsh immigration bill
evidence.finding: Naval blockade authority; SAR NGO criminalization; refoulement risk via Libya/Tunisia coast guard co-funding.
Migration bill advances with naval-blockade authority.
Milan municipal pushback; subnational mitigation signal.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 3.0 confirmed under pressure. Subnational (Milan) recognition partially counterbalances national-level deterrence framing.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 3.0 confirmed under pressure. Naval-blockade authority converts response from rescue-priority to deterrence-priority.
scoring_rationale.BND: 3.5 confirmed under significant pressure. Naval-blockade authority and SAR criminalization cross fundamental refoulement boundaries; documented downward pressure but not yet sufficient to drop a half-step.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 3.0 confirmed under pressure. Albania offshore plan court rejections demonstrate residual constitutional check.
scoring_rationale.INT: 3.0 confirmed under pressure. EU/CoE / 1951 Refugee Convention commitments contradicted by criminalization of rescue. Documented downward pressure.

math_check.validator_check: Reconstruction matches published 50.0 exactly. No math-hygiene flag.

negativeSignals: Naval-blockade authority; SAR NGO criminalization (50K euro fines, boat seizures); Libya/Tunisia coast guard co-funding with documented abuse records; HRW 'Puts Lives at Risk' framing; refoulement risk.
positiveSignals: Italian courts repeatedly rejected Albania offshore detention plan; Milan municipal pushback; functioning constitutional review; EU framework engagement; refugee absorption (1M+ over decades); active humanitarian civil society.
sector_context: EU migration-policy compound cluster with Greece (parallel proposal documents Feb 5 criminalization of rescue and Feb 3 Coast Guard-dinghy collision deaths). Italy and Greece together represent EU southern-border deterrence-pivot; both never assessed.
open_actions_resolved: Feb 2026 migration bill, Libya/Tunisia coast guard co-funding, Albania court rejections absorbed
open_actions_carried: 60-day re-evaluation: bill operationalization and SAR criminalization enforcement
Watch: any constitutional court ruling on naval-blockade authority
